hero.title: Average Bathroom Renovation Cost in Vancouver (2026 Price Guide)
hero.subtitle: The average bathroom renovation cost in Vancouver ranges from $10,000 to $60,000+. These are real prices from 19 completed projects — not industry estimates or national averages.

tiers.title: Bathroom Renovation Cost by Tier
tiers.subtitle: Based on our completed projects across Greater Vancouver

budget.title: Budget-Friendly
budget.description: Standard tile, basic vanity, updated fixtures, and fresh paint. Ideal for a single bathroom refresh in condos or townhouses without moving plumbing.

midRange.title: Mid-Range
midRange.description: Porcelain or large-format tile, custom vanity, frameless glass shower enclosure, modern fixtures, and heated flooring. The most popular choice for family homes.

highEnd.title: High-End / Multi-Bathroom
highEnd.description: Premium tile, curbless showers, freestanding tubs, custom cabinetry, smart fixtures, and structural modifications. Often includes renovating 2-3+ bathrooms simultaneously.

factors.title: What Affects Your Bathroom Renovation Cost?
factors.subtitle: Six key factors that determine your final price

tiles.title: Tile & Flooring (25–35% of budget)
tiles.description: Standard ceramic tile starts at $5/sq ft, porcelain runs $8–15/sq ft, and natural stone can exceed $25/sq ft. Large-format tiles reduce grout lines but cost more to install.

shower.title: Shower / Tub Configuration
shower.description: A tub-to-shower conversion costs $3,000–8,000. Curbless (zero-threshold) showers require custom waterproofing and sloped subfloor, adding $2,000–5,000 vs a standard shower base.

vanity.title: Vanity & Countertop
vanity.description: Stock vanities start at $500–1,500. Semi-custom with quartz top runs $2,000–4,000. Fully custom floating vanities with integrated sinks can exceed $6,000.

plumbing.title: Plumbing Relocation
plumbing.description: Moving drain lines or supply pipes adds $2,000–6,000. Older homes may need new shut-off valves or supply line upgrades. Keeping existing plumbing layout saves significantly.

waterproofing.title: Waterproofing
waterproofing.description: Proper waterproofing with membrane systems (Schluter, Laticrete) costs $1,500–3,500 for a standard shower. Essential in Vancouver's climate to prevent moisture damage behind walls.

permits.title: Permits & Inspections
permits.description: Building permits for bathroom renovations cost $200–800 in Metro Vancouver. Required when moving plumbing, adding new fixtures, or making structural changes.

realProjects.title: Real Bathroom Renovation Projects
realProjects.subtitle: Browse {count} completed bathroom projects with actual budgets and timelines

tips.title: Money-Saving Tips for Your Bathroom Renovation

tip1.title: Keep Existing Plumbing Layout
tip1.description: Relocating drain lines is one of the biggest cost drivers. Keeping the toilet, shower, and sink in their current positions can save $3,000–6,000.

tip2.title: Choose Large-Format Porcelain Tile
tip2.description: Fewer grout lines mean less labour and a cleaner look. 24x24 or 12x24 porcelain tiles are durable, water-resistant, and more affordable than natural stone.

tip3.title: Renovate Multiple Bathrooms Together
tip3.description: If you need more than one bathroom done, doing them simultaneously saves 10–20% vs separate projects. Contractors can share mobilization costs and bulk-buy materials.

tip4.title: Invest in Waterproofing
tip4.description: Skimping on waterproofing leads to costly repairs later. Proper membrane systems add $1,500–3,500 upfront but prevent $10,000+ in water damage down the road.

cityComparison.subtitle: How budgets vary across Metro Vancouver based on our completed projects
cityComparison.footnote: Cities not listed are still serviced — we just have not completed enough bathroom-only projects there to publish a meaningful range yet.

questions.title: Questions to Ask Your Bathroom Renovation Contractor
questions.subtitle: Use these to compare quotes apples-to-apples and avoid surprises mid-project

q1.title: Is plumbing relocation included in your quote?
q1.description: Moving a drain or supply line typically adds $2,000–$6,000. Contractors who quote without specifying often add it later as a change order. Confirm in writing whether the toilet, shower drain, and vanity supply lines are staying in place.

q2.title: What waterproofing system do you use?
q2.description: Reputable bathroom installs use a tested membrane (Schluter Kerdi, Laticrete Hydro Ban, or equivalent). If the contractor names a generic 'liquid waterproofing' or skips the question, walk away — water damage is the most expensive bathroom failure mode.

q3.title: Are permits and inspections included?
q3.description: Most Metro Vancouver bathrooms need a plumbing permit when fixtures are added or relocated. Permit fees run $200–$800. Confirm whether the contractor handles permit applications and rough-in/final inspections, or whether you are responsible.

q4.title: Who is the actual installation crew?
q4.description: Some companies sub the entire job to a third-party crew you never meet. Ask whether the people doing demolition, framing, plumbing, tile, and finish work are employees, long-term subcontractors, or one-time hires. Continuity affects warranty follow-through.

q5.title: What is the workmanship warranty and what does it cover?
q5.description: Industry standard is 1 year. Quality contractors offer 2–3 years and explicitly cover tile cracking, grout failure, leaking shower pans, vanity drawer alignment, and paint finish. Get warranty terms in writing before signing.

q6.title: What is your payment schedule and milestone breakdown?
q6.description: Avoid contractors asking for >30% upfront. Healthy schedules are 30% deposit, 30% at rough-in, 30% at tile/cabinet install, 10% at final inspection. Milestone payments protect both parties if scope or quality issues arise.

faq.q1: How much does a bathroom renovation cost in Vancouver?
faq.a1: Bathroom renovation costs in Vancouver range from $10,000 to $60,000+ based on our completed projects. A single budget-friendly bathroom starts around $10,000–$20,000, mid-range renovations with custom vanity and glass shower run $20,000–$35,000, and high-end or multi-bathroom projects can exceed $40,000.
faq.q2: How long does a bathroom renovation take?
faq.a2: A single bathroom renovation typically takes 2–5 weeks. Simple cosmetic updates can be done in 2–3 weeks, while projects involving plumbing relocation, curbless showers, or structural changes may take 4–8 weeks. Multi-bathroom projects take 5–11 weeks.
faq.q3: Can I convert my bathtub to a walk-in shower?
faq.a3: Yes, tub-to-shower conversions are one of our most popular requests. The cost ranges from $3,000–$8,000 depending on whether you choose a standard shower base or curbless design. Curbless showers require custom waterproofing and a sloped subfloor.
faq.q4: What is curbless shower and why is it more expensive?
faq.a4: A curbless (zero-threshold) shower has no step or lip at the entry, creating a seamless floor transition. It requires custom waterproofing membrane, a precisely sloped subfloor, and linear drain installation, adding $2,000–$5,000 vs a standard shower base. It is popular for accessibility and modern aesthetics.
faq.q5: Should I renovate multiple bathrooms at the same time?
faq.a5: Yes, renovating multiple bathrooms simultaneously saves 10–20% compared to separate projects. Contractors can share mobilization costs, coordinate trades more efficiently, and buy materials in bulk. We have completed many 2–3 bathroom projects across Greater Vancouver.
